note: 16x16 grid, no gutter. Sheets whose pixel height is not a multiple of 16 have their partial bottom row dropped; see sheets[].partial.

roles.fill_plain: one solid colour, zero pixel detail - renders as a flat background wash, not as art
roles.fill_textured: full-tile tileable ground fill that HAS visible detail (specks, tufts, ripples, stones) - use these for ground
roles.transition_edge: edge/corner piece where one terrain meets another; only correct next to the matching fill
roles.object_part: one slice of a sprite that spans several tiles - never usable alone
roles.object_whole: a sprite that fits entirely inside its tile
roles.decor: small self-contained detail to scatter over a fill

partial.note: dimension not a multiple of 16; only whole rows/cols indexed

pick.name: grass_lime_textured
pick.reason: lime grass with scattered blade tufts - the standard overworld grass, and the fix for the flat 0,12 currently wired into build_atlas.py

runner_up.name: grass_green_textured
runner_up.reason: same tufted pattern in a deeper forest green, for woodland regions

pick.name: earth_taupe_textured
pick.reason: taupe earth with fine grain speckle; reads as packed soil rather than a brown wash

runner_up.name: earth_taupe_textured
runner_up.reason: same taupe base with a sparser grain - use as the second dirt tile so runs of dirt do not visibly repeat

pick.name: sand_textured
pick.reason: orange sand with wind-blown squiggle detail; matches the sand shoreline set in TilesetWater cols 0-9 rows 0-4

runner_up.name: sand_dune_ripple_textured
runner_up.reason: paler golden dune sand carrying long wind ripples, from the camp mesa

pick.name: snow_textured
pick.reason: white snow with faint crystal flecks - keeps snow reading as a surface, not as page background

runner_up.name: snow_textured
runner_up.reason: same snow base with heavier flecking - alternate so large snowfields do not repeat

pick.name: grass_green_textured
pick.reason: deep green densely tufted grass - the pack has no dedicated marsh fill, and this is the wettest-looking ground in it; pair with the swamp shoreline at TilesetWater cols 13-23 rows 6-10

runner_up.name: earth_taupe_textured
runner_up.reason: taupe grained earth, for the drier mud-flat half of a marsh

pick.name: floor_cobble_dark_textured
pick.reason: dark cobble in near-black mortar - actually grey and broken up, unlike the flat editor swatch TilesetLogic 6,0 that was previously used for ash

runner_up.name: floor_cobble_dark_textured
runner_up.reason: the darkest cobble variant of the same set, for burnt-out cores

pick.name: floor_cobble_sand_textured
pick.reason: sandy cobblestone with a clear stone-by-stone pattern that still reads at 16px

runner_up.name: floor_brick_pale_textured
runner_up.reason: pale stone brick paving - use where stone should read as built rather than natural

pick.name: water_textured
pick.reason: open water with diagonal sparkle streaks; moves the eye, unlike the flat cyan at 11,0

runner_up.name: water_ice_textured
runner_up.reason: pale ice-water with the same streaks, for cold regions

pick.name: water_shore_grass_edge_ns
pick.reason: water-to-grass shoreline with the white foam rim; this cell is the top edge of the full 10x5 autotile at cols 0-9 rows 6-10 - take the whole block, not one tile

runner_up.name: water_shore_sand_edge_ring
runner_up.reason: water-to-sand shoreline, identical autotile shape at cols 0-9 rows 0-4
